message:
fix(bash): remove double quotes from associative array keys to fix set -u

In bash associative array declarations with `set -u` (nounset), using
double-quoted keys like `["user_setup"]` causes bash to interpret the
content as a variable reference and attempt to expand `$user_setup`.

This was causing the installer to fail with:
  /tmp/acfs-state-*.sh: line 1318: user_setup: unbound variable

The fix is to use unquoted keys: `[user_setup]="value"` which bash
treats as literal strings.

Files fixed:
- scripts/lib/state.sh: ACFS_PHASE_NAMES array (9 keys)
- scripts/lib/security.sh: KNOWN_INSTALLERS array (15 keys)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
